name: OWASP AI Security Landscape
description: A comprehensive overview of OWASP resources for AI and machine learning security.

children.name: OWASP Generative AI
children.description: The OWASP GenAI Security Project — a global community delivering peer-reviewed guidance on securing LLMs, generative AI, and agentic systems across multiple focused initiatives.

children.name: Agentic Security Initiative
children.description: Focused on the unique security challenges of autonomous AI and agentic systems that plan, act, and make decisions across complex workflows.

children.title: Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
children.description: A globally peer-reviewed framework identifying the most critical security risks for autonomous and agentic AI systems, developed with 100+ industry experts including NIST and European Commission representatives.

children.title: Agentic AI Threats and Mitigations
children.description: A threat-model-based reference of emerging agentic threats and mitigations — the first in a series from the Agentic Security Initiative covering autonomous AI risks.

children.title: Securing Agentic Applications Guide 1.0
children.description: Practical, actionable guidance for designing, developing, and deploying secure LLM-powered agentic applications — complements the Threats and Mitigations taxonomy with concrete technical recommendations.

children.title: AI Security Solutions Landscape for Agentic AI Q2 2026
children.description: Quarterly peer-reviewed map of the agentic AI security vendor ecosystem, covering the full DevOps-SecOps intersection and mapping solutions to agentic threats and mitigations.

children.name: GenAI Red Teaming Initiative
children.description: Establishing standardized AI red teaming methodologies, benchmarks, and evaluation frameworks for LLMs — addressing security vulnerabilities, bias, and user trust.

children.title: GenAI Red Teaming Guide
children.description: Comprehensive strategies for identifying and mitigating security risks in AI-driven systems through red teaming, covering model evaluation, implementation testing, infrastructure assessment, and runtime behavior analysis.

children.title: Vendor Evaluation Criteria for AI Red Teaming
children.description: Practical guide for organizations assessing vendors offering AI red teaming services or automated testing tools — covering green flags, red flags, threat models, evaluation rigor, tooling quality, and governance considerations.

children.name: Secure AI Adoption and Governance
children.description: Forming a Center of Excellence to enhance security frameworks, governance policies, and cross-departmental collaboration for safe and ethical LLM adoption.

children.title: LLM Applications Cybersecurity and Governance Checklist
children.description: Practical checklist for leaders across executive, tech, cybersecurity, compliance, and legal domains — covering governance, risk, and compliance for LLM deployment.

children.title: Threat Defense COMPASS
children.description: A spreadsheet-based AI threat resilience strategy dashboard that consolidates AI threats, vulnerabilities, defenses, and mitigations to guide rapid threat prioritization and strategic decision-making.

children.title: GenAI Security Solutions Reference Guide
children.description: Vendor-agnostic resource mapping identified LLM and agentic AI risks to practical open-source and commercial security solutions across planning, data handling, deployment, and monitoring stages.

children.name: LLM and GenAI Resources
children.description: Flagship resources from the OWASP GenAI Security Project covering the LLM Top 10, supply chain security, and AI security tooling.

children.title: Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025
children.description: The most critical security risks for applications built on large language models — from prompt injection to model theft — the industry-leading reference for LLM security.

children.title: AI Security Solutions Landscape (LLM)
children.description: Vendor-agnostic overview of the security tooling ecosystem for LLM and generative AI applications across the full model lifecycle.

children.title: AIBOM Generator
children.description: Open-source tool for generating AI Bills of Materials (AIBOMs) to enhance AI supply chain transparency, traceability, and security.

children.title: Practical Guide for Secure MCP Server Development
children.description: Actionable guidance for securing Model Context Protocol servers — the critical connection point between AI assistants and external tools, APIs, and data sources.

children.title: Cheat Sheet: Securely Using Third-Party MCP Servers
children.description: Security framework for safely deploying external MCP servers, covering tool poisoning, prompt injection, authentication, authorization, and least-privilege governance workflows.

children.name: AI Data Security Initiative
children.description: Focused on the unique data security challenges introduced by generative AI — covering training data, inference pipelines, RAG systems, and data governance for LLM deployments.

children.title: GenAI Data Security Risks and Mitigations 2026
children.description: Forward-looking analysis of 21 distinct data security risk categories (DSGAI01-DSGAI21) for LLMs and agentic AI, covering attack scenarios, real-world CVEs, and tiered mitigations for the AI data layer.

children.title: LLM and GenAI Data Security Best Practices
children.description: Comprehensive best practices addressing data security challenges in LLM deployments, covering breaches of sensitive information and data protection regulation compliance.

children.name: AI Threat Intelligence Initiative
children.description: Researching how LLMs are exploited in offensive cyber operations, including exploit generation capabilities, deepfake threats, and AI-assisted attack detection.

children.title: Guide for Preparing and Responding to Deepfake Events
children.description: Resilience-focused guidance for defending against deepfake-enhanced social engineering — emphasizing process adherence, strong financial controls, and a culture of verification over detection technology.

children.title: Securing and Scrutinizing LLMs in Exploit Generation
children.description: Research evaluating the real-world offensive capabilities of LLMs such as GPT-4o, Claude, and DeepSeek for generating day-one vulnerability exploits and assessing detection capabilities.

children.title: GenAI Incident Response Guide 1.0
children.description: Guidelines and best practices for responding to security incidents involving GenAI applications, covering attacks on AI systems, supply chains, and third-party model providers — aligned with the NIST IR lifecycle.

children.name: OWASP AI Exchange
children.description: OWASP Flagship project providing an open, comprehensive knowledge base for AI security — covering threats, controls, testing, and privacy across the full AI lifecycle.

children.title: AI Security Overview
children.description: Foundational material covering the AI threat landscape, control frameworks, risk analysis methodologies, governance essentials, and organizational security structures.

children.title: General Controls
children.description: Controls every organisation should have in place — covering governance frameworks, data limitation strategies, and mechanisms for limiting unwanted AI behaviors.

children.title: Threats Through Use (Input Threats)
children.description: Guidance on adversarial attacks through crafted inputs: evasion, prompt injection, data disclosure, model extraction, resource exhaustion, and alignment risks.

children.title: Development-Time Threats and Controls
children.description: Attacks targeting the AI development environment and supply chain — including model poisoning, development data leaks, and supply chain compromise prevention.

children.title: Runtime Application Security Threats
children.description: Operational security concerns including runtime model poisoning, direct model leakage, output injection, and manipulation of augmentation data in production.

children.title: AI Security Testing
children.description: Red-teaming methodologies, vulnerability simulation approaches, and open-source tool recommendations for security testing both generative and predictive AI systems.

children.title: AI Privacy
children.description: Privacy responsibilities across AI systems — use limitation, fairness, data minimization, privacy rights, consent, model-based privacy attacks, and transparency requirements.

children.name: OWASP AISVS
children.description: The AI Security Verification Standard — a framework of security requirements for designing, building, and testing AI/ML systems across 14 control categories.

children.title: C1: Training Data Integrity and Traceability
children.description: Training data must be sourced, handled, and maintained to preserve origin traceability, integrity, and quality — guarding against tampering, poisoning, and corruption.

children.title: C2: User Input Validation
children.description: Robust validation of user input as a first-line defense against prompt injection, jailbreaks, and context-window exploitation in AI systems.

children.title: C3: Model Lifecycle Management
children.description: Change control processes preventing unauthorized or unsafe model modifications from reaching production, covering the full lifecycle from development to decommissioning.

children.title: C4: Infrastructure and Deployment Security
children.description: Hardening AI infrastructure against privilege escalation, supply chain tampering, and lateral movement through secure configuration, runtime isolation, and trusted deployment pipelines.

children.title: C5: Access Control and Identity
children.description: Robust identity management and context-aware authorization under zero-trust principles, ensuring humans, services, and agents operate only within explicitly granted scopes.

children.title: C6: Supply Chain Security
children.description: End-to-end traceability, vulnerability management, and monitoring to protect against backdoors and exploitable code in third-party models, frameworks, and datasets.

children.title: C7: Model Behavior and Output Control
children.description: Technical constraints, validation, and monitoring of model outputs so unsafe, malformed, or high-risk responses cannot reach users or downstream systems.

children.title: C8: Memory, Embeddings and Vector DB Security
children.description: Hardening of RAG memory pipelines and vector databases against embedding inversion, membership inference, cross-tenant leakage, and data exfiltration.

children.title: C9: Orchestration and Agentic Action Security
children.description: Ensuring autonomous and multi-agent systems execute only authorized, intended, and bounded actions — preventing tool misuse, privilege escalation, and uncontrolled recursion.

children.title: C10: MCP Security
children.description: Secure discovery, authentication, authorization, and transport for Model Context Protocol integrations — preventing context confusion, unauthorized tool invocation, and cross-tenant data exposure.

children.title: C11: Adversarial Robustness
children.description: Keeping AI systems reliable and abuse-resistant against evasion, inference, extraction, and poisoning attacks through alignment testing, adversarial hardening, and model theft deterrence.

children.title: C12: Privacy Protection
children.description: Rigorous privacy assurances across the AI lifecycle — collection, training, inference, and incident response — with minimum necessary scope, consent, and provable erasure.

children.title: C13: Monitoring, Logging and Anomaly Detection
children.description: Real-time and forensic visibility into what the model and AI components see, do, and return — enabling threat detection, triage, and learning from incidents.

children.title: C14: Human Oversight and Governance
children.description: Maintaining human oversight and clear accountability chains in AI systems, ensuring explainability, transparency, and ethical stewardship throughout the AI lifecycle.

children.name: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series
children.description: AI-specific cheat sheets from the OWASP Cheat Sheet Series covering LLM prompt injection, agent security, and secure AI operations.

children.title: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention
children.description: Practical mitigations for prompt injection attacks — one of the most critical vulnerabilities in LLM-powered applications, with concrete defense patterns.

children.title: AI Agent Security
children.description: Security best practices for autonomous AI agent systems: tool permissions, input validation, human oversight, memory poisoning defenses, and multi-agent communication safeguards.

children.title: Secure AI and ML Model Ops
children.description: Practical MLOps security guidance covering data poisoning, model extraction, adversarial inputs, and prompt injection across development, deployment, and operational phases.

children.name: OWASP AI Testing Guide
children.description: A structured framework and methodology for security testing AI systems — covering threat modeling, application testing, model testing, infrastructure testing, and data testing.

children.title: Threat Modeling for AI Systems
children.description: Structured process for identifying and quantifying security threats to AI systems, covering prompt injection, model extraction, and other ML-specific attack paths using architectural threat modeling.

children.title: Identify AI Threats
children.description: Architectural scoped threat modeling approach for AI-enabled applications near production, structured around Google's Secure AI Framework (SAIF) components for holistic risk-driven vulnerability mapping.

children.title: AI Testing Guide Framework
children.description: The unified testing framework bridging traditional cybersecurity, MLOps testing, and Responsible AI assessments across four pillars: application, model, infrastructure, and data testing.

children.title: AI Application Testing
children.description: Security and trust testing for AI system interactions with users and external data sources — covering prompt injection, sensitive data leaks, unsafe outputs, agentic misbehavior, and model extraction.

children.title: AI Model Testing
children.description: Testing intrinsic model properties under adversarial conditions — detecting susceptibility to evasion attacks, data poisoning, privacy leaks, and misalignment independent of deployment context.

children.title: AI Infrastructure Testing
children.description: Security testing of AI deployment infrastructure — model supply chains, resource management, boundary controls, fine-tuning environments, and prevention of unauthorized model access or misuse.

children.title: AI Data Testing
children.description: Validation and protection of data across the AI lifecycle — training datasets, inference inputs, and runtime interactions — covering privacy, dataset diversity, compliance, and harmful content prevention.

children.name: OWASP AIMA
children.description: The AI Maturity Assessment framework — helping organizations evaluate and improve their AI practices across five domains: Strategy, Design, Implementation, Operations, and Governance.

children.title: Strategy Domain
children.description: Maturity assessment for aligning AI systems and initiatives with organizational objectives, risk appetite, and long-term direction.

children.title: Design Domain
children.description: Maturity assessment for responsible AI system architecture — covering safety, fairness, explainability, and security requirements at the design stage.

children.title: Implementation Domain
children.description: Maturity assessment for building and deploying AI solutions — covering secure development practices, data pipelines, model training, and validation processes.

children.title: Operations Domain
children.description: Maturity assessment for managing live AI systems — covering monitoring, incident response, drift detection, and continuous improvement in production environments.

children.title: Governance Domain
children.description: Maturity assessment for AI oversight, accountability, and compliance — integrating OWASP SAMM, ISO/IEC standards, and cross-disciplinary governance frameworks.

children.title: AIMA Assessment Toolkit v1.0
children.description: Practical Excel-based toolkit for conducting the AI Maturity Assessment, providing structured scoring across all five domains with actionable maturity roadmap guidance.
